message:
fix(metrics): use raw wall-clock for tantivy_last_update_us, not the skew-protected clock

`SearchMetrics::update_index_health` previously recorded
`now_micros()`, which routes through the monotonic skew guard that
refuses to move time backwards (so that cross-thread mailbox
ordering invariants hold). Inside the metrics path this is the
wrong choice: when the skew guard has been pushed into the far
future by an earlier call (or by a deliberately poisoned
`LAST_SYSTEM_TIME_US` in tests), the "last index update" gauge
snaps to that future timestamp and stays there for the lifetime of
the process, making dashboards / alerts show an index that was
"just rebuilt 10_000 s from now".

Switch the metrics write to `now_micros_raw`, which returns the
real wall-clock reading. The skew guard still protects the message
ordering path where it belongs; observability gauges now reflect
the actual local clock, which is what Prometheus/Grafana need to
decorrelate "clock moved" from "indexer did work".

Test update: `search_metrics_update_index_health_uses_raw_timestamp`
(renamed) now asserts that the recorded metric lies inside
`[now_micros_raw() before call, now_micros_raw() after call]` after
the skew guard has been poisoned with a +10_000 s future value,
pinning the invariant "metrics gauges follow wall clock, not the
monotonic guard".

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
